policy: Expanded snippets do not promote candidates. They only strengthen display and audit of rows already in authoritative_scope_rows.

records.snippet_en: n P Net i -Ze l r l o P a illars r 1: Decarbonizing Our Operations continued The chart below outlines the year-over-year evolution of our Scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions, our base year results and associated intensity metrics. For additional information on our progress, refer to the Scope 1 and 2 Target section. Scope 1 and 2 GHG Emissions: Breakdown by Approach, Scope and Intensity Metrics1 1 See Our Approach to Calculating Non-Financed GHG Emissions for details on our organizational boundary, reporting period, and other key details related to our footprint. Totals may not add up due to rounding. Units 2024 2023 2022 20193 Scope 1 and 2 (Location-Based)2 Scope 14 tCO2e 39,292 40,346 43,707 52,628 Scope 25 tCO2e 76,179 76,970 79,615 110,221 Total Scope 1 and 2 tCO2e P erformance indicators for which EY provided a limited level of assurance 115,472 P erformance indicators for which EY provided a limited level of assurance 117,317 P erformance indicators for which EY provided a limited level of assurance 123,322 P erformance indicators for which EY provided a limited level of assurance 162,849 Facility GHG Emissions Intensity kgCO2e per square foot 5.1 5.3 5.3 6.7 Revenue GHG Emissions Intensity tCO2e per millions in revenue 2.0 2.3 2.4 3.8 Scope 1 and 2 (Market-Based)2 Scope 14 tCO2e 39,292 40,346 43,707 52,628 Scope 25,6 tCO2e 5,350 6,930 6,977 8,100 Total Scope 1 and 2 tCO2e P erformance indicators for which EY provided a limited level of assurance 44,642 P erformance indicators

records.snippet_en: ss travel – GHG emissions from the transportation of employees for business-related activities in vehicles owned or operated by third parties (e.g., aircraft, trains, etc.). • Category 8: Upstream leased assets – GHG emissions from the operation of assets leased by TD in the reporting year and not included in Scope 1 or 2. • Category 13: Downstream leased assets – includes GHG emissions from the operation of assets that are owned by TD and leased to other entities that are not included in Scope 1 or 2. The chart below outlines the year-over-year evolution of our Scope 3 non-financed GHG emissions, our base year results and associated intensity metrics. Category Unit 20241 2023 2022 2019 Purchased Goods and Services tCO2e 1,144,923 1,053,692 1,058,672 627,428 Capital Goods tCO2e 104,032 71,736 74,060 156,813 Fuel- and Energy-Related Activities tCO2e 22,231 22,882 24,515 32,166 Business Travel2,3 tCO2e P erformance indicators for which EY provided a limited level of assurance 28,807 P erformance indicators for which EY provided a limited level of assurance 28,147 P erformance indicators for which EY provided a limited level of assurance 12,092 P erformance indicators for which EY provided a limited level of assurance 17,154 Upstream Leased Assets4 tCO2e P erformance indicators for which EY provided a limited level of assurance 391 – – – Downstream Leased Assets5 tCO2e P erformance indicators for which EY provided a limited level of assurance 412 P erformance indicators for whic
